title: Dallas · Two-Thirds Down
subtitle: FBI UCR 1985–2014 + DPD incidents 2015–2026 · 8 police divisions · real locations

hook.line: Dallas crime, from the 1988 peak to 2014
hook.sub: FBI UCR · then the map, month by month, up close

punchline.sub: −68% under the same FBI measure · a newer, broader count begins in 2015

copy.seamExplain: The ruler changes here — not the city. Through 2014 the gray marks are the FBI's classic 'index' crimes (murder, robbery, assault, burglary, theft). From 2015 the figures are DPD's own incident records — a broader count, so the level steps up. One more caveat, disclosed: DPD's public dataset excludes sex offenses and juvenile cases entirely. Compare the shape within one color, never across the dashed line.
copy.chapter2Kicker: CHAPTER 2 · THE LAST 5 YEARS · DPD OPEN DATA
copy.chapter2Caption: Monthly reports, 2021–2026. Every dot is a real reported incident location, geocoded by DPD, sampled.
copy.transitionDesc: The chart counted per year. From here the map counts per month across 8 police divisions — every dot a real reported location, geocoded and published by DPD.
copy.methodRecentSub: the last five years · month by month · real locations
copy.methodDotsSub: Every dot is a real incident location, geocoded and published by DPD. 99.9% map to one of 8 police divisions — the rest counted, disclosed, never invented.
copy.methodFootnote: FBI UCR (through 2014) and DPD incidents (2015+) are different measures — shown as two chapters, not one line. DPD's public dataset omits sex offenses and juvenile cases entirely.
copy.quizQuestion: Which Dallas police division reports the fewest Group A crimes?
copy.sourceLine: Data: Dallas Police Department via the Dallas Open Data portal (ODC-BY) · sex offenses & juvenile cases excluded by the source
copy.coverageText: 99.9% of records mapped to a police division · source omits sex offenses & juvenile cases
copy.creditsSources: Sources: Dallas Police Department incident data (2015–present) via the Dallas Open Data portal, licensed ODC-BY (attribution: Dallas Police Department), & FBI UCR (1985–2014) via the FBI Crime Data Explorer (ORI TXDPD0000). Police divisions: official DPD boundaries (8). DPD filters this dataset before release: sexually oriented offenses and juvenile-involved cases never appear, so every total undercounts actual reported crime — disclosed on screen. FBI UCR index crimes and DPD incidents are different measures, shown as two chapters and never equated. Basemap: © OpenStreetMap contributors (ODbL).

historyNotes.text: 1988: the peak — 171,772 index crimes reported in a single year.
2014: 54,511 — the last FBI year, down 68% from 1988; DPD's own record begins next year.
2021: 114,352 — the high point of DPD's newer, broader count.

annotations.text: Every dot is a real reported incident location — geocoded and published by DPD open data.
July 2023 — the busiest Group A month of the last five years: 7,460.
Northeast — Dallas's busiest division for reported Group A crime over the last five years.
North Central: down 25% since 2022 — the city's steepest division drop.
Reported Group A is falling citywide: 78,779 (2022) → 67,314 (2025), −15%.
